name: Roborock Qrevo Curv Robot Vacuum and Mop

specs.Mop System: DuoDivide dual parallel short-bristle rollers (Curv-exclusive), 200 RPM, 10mm auto-lift
specs.Main Brush: All-rubber anti-tangle spiral brush (0% SGS-certified tangle rate)
specs.Side Brush: FlexiArm extending side brush (reaches beyond robot body for edge cleaning)
specs.Dock Functions: Multifunctional Dock 3.0: auto dust empty, hot-water mop wash at 75°C, warm-air dry at 45°C, auto water refill

whatWorks: 0% pet hair tangle on the main brush is the most consistently cited reason owners choose the Curv over competing models — nearly every owner review with pets mentions this, and it held up to Vacuum Wars lab testing as tied best in class across 100+ models.

The FlexiArm extending side brush reaches past the robot's body to clean along baseboards and furniture edges — owners who previously used fixed-arm robots call this out as the single biggest day-to-day difference.

Carpet deep clean score of 86/100 in Vacuum Wars testing (tied 6th of 100+ models) combined with AdaptiLift Chassis handling for high thresholds are recurring notes from owners with mixed hard floor and carpet homes.

The Multifunctional Dock 3.0's hot-water mop wash and warm-air drying cycle come up in nearly every owner review as the feature that justifies the premium — mop hygiene and kitchen-floor cleanliness are the specific examples cited.
worthKnowing: Obstacle avoidance uses Reactive Tech structured light rather than a full AI camera system — a step up from the QV 35A but owners note it still struggles with flat cables and very small objects on the floor.
Vacuum Wars placed the Curv as their previous #1 robot vacuum before newer 2026 models arrived — as of May 2026 it remains a top-5 pick, competitive but no longer the outright leader.

The DuoDivide dual parallel brush system is Curv-exclusive and works differently from single-roller designs — owners switching from older Roborocks note the carpet cleaning pattern is noticeably more thorough.

commonComplaints.title: Poor obstacle avoidance is the top…
commonComplaints.body: Poor obstacle avoidance is the top negative raised in Reddit discussions — owners in r/RobotVacuums specifically note that smaller items (cables, small toys, socks) are not reliably detected and the robot runs over or pushes them.

commonComplaints.title: Airflow performance is flagged in Vacuum…
commonComplaints.body: Airflow performance is flagged in Vacuum Wars testing as average for the premium tier — they note this does not impact daily cleaning noticeably but matters in direct suction-only comparisons against models with optimized airflow.

commonComplaints.title: Reddit sentiment tracked on RedditRecs shows…
commonComplaints.body: Reddit sentiment tracked on RedditRecs shows the side brush spreading debris in extreme corner conditions during testing — owners rarely raise this in regular use, but it appears in controlled lab scenarios.

verdict: Owners who have used the Qrevo Curv for more than a few weeks consistently describe it as the point where robot vacuuming becomes genuinely low-maintenance rather than just convenient. The 0% hair tangle, FlexiArm edge reach, and hot-water mop dock are the three things cited in real owner write-ups as reasons they stopped considering other models. The obstacle avoidance gap is real and comes up in Reddit threads, but for owners who declutter before runs or set no-go zones, it does not override overall satisfaction.

realBuyers.quote: The FlexiArm side brush actually gets into corners. Every other robot I've had leaves a strip along the wall. This one doesn't.
Dog hair is picked up completely every run. I have two golden retrievers and I have not once had to cut hair off the brush. That alone justifies the price.
The mopping is better than I expected. It actually cleans my kitchen floor, not just makes it wet. The dock washes the pads in hot water and they smell clean after.

sources.title: Vacuum Wars: Roborock Qrevo Curv Review
RedditRecs: Roborock Qrevo Curv — All Reddit Reviews

metaDescription: Roborock Qrevo Curv review for Canadian buyers: 18,500Pa suction, FlexiArm edge mopping, DuoDivide brush, 0% pet hair tangle. Real owner feedback and Canadian price ($849.99).
introText: The Roborock Qrevo Curv is a 2024 premium robot vacuum with 18,500Pa HyperForce suction, a Curv-exclusive DuoDivide dual-roller brush system, FlexiArm extending side brush for edge mopping, and Multifunctional Dock 3.0 with hot-water mop washing at 75°C. In Vacuum Wars lab testing it tied 1st for 0% pet hair tangle across 100+ models. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B0D81675BQ).
